name: San Francisco Sourdough French Bread
recipeIngredient: 5/8 cup Water (for Welbilt/Dak - no water needed)
2 teaspoons Red Star yeast (except for machines - use 4 ts)
recipeInstructions:
Recipe for a 1.5 lb loaf.
For mild sourdough:
1. Place all ingredients except cornmeal in the bread machine pan.
2. Select Dough setting and press Start.
For sourest sourdough:
1. In a medium bowl, combine water, starter (room temperature), and half of the flour.
2. Cover and let stand in a warm place until very sour and bubbly, from 24 to 48 hours.
3. Combine mixture with the rest of the ingredients except cornmeal in the bread machine pan.
4. Select Dough setting and press Start. When dough has risen enough, the machine will beep.
5. Turn off bread machine, remove bread pan, and turn out dough on a floured countertop or cutting board.
6. Shape dough into one 12-inch oblong loaf, one large round loaf, two 18-inch thin baguettes, or eight French rolls.
7. Place loaf (loaves) on a baking sheet well greased or sprinkled with cornmeal.
8. Slash the rolls or baguettes straight down the center about 1/2 inch deep.
9. On the oblong loaf, make 3 diagonal slashes; on the round loaf, slash an X or # on top.
10. Let rise in a warm oven for 30-45 minutes until doubled (Hint: turn oven on Warm for 2 minutes, then off, and place covered dough in oven to rise; remove pan from oven to preheat it).
11. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
12. Mist each loaf with water using a plant mister (or brush with water) at 5-minute intervals during baking.
13. Bake for 25-30 minutes until golden brown.
14. Remove from oven and cool on wire racks.
15. To preserve the crisp crust, do not store in plastic wrap or bags; loosely cover or leave out for up to 2 days before it dries out completely.
